u) It is settled principle that in the matters of restitution, the courts should adopt pragmatic
view and grant relief in a manner as may be reasonable, fair and practicable.

It has been
Order in Petition No. 78/MP/2023 Page 10 of 31held that the Court should not be oblivious of any unmerited hardship to be suffered by
the party against whom action by way of restitution is taken.

v) The Petitioner should be required to establish to the satisfaction of the Commission that
it has made prudent and bona-fide effort to minimise the interest cost.

obligations.obligation:
(h) During the pendency of the above Petition, the Central Government, in
exercise of powers under Section 176 of the Act, has on 22.10.2021 notified
the Electricity (Timely Recovery of Costs due to Change in Law) Rules, 2021
(hereinafter referred to as “the Change in Law Rules”).

The Respondent No. 4, ERBPL vide its written submissions dated 4.2.2022 has
additionally submitted as under:
…
(f) While it has been admitted by the Petitioner that the Change in Law Rules
provide for adjustment in tariff on occurrence of Change in Law and specifically
entitle the affected party to ...

As per sub-rule (2) of Rule 1, these Rules shall come into
force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette.
obligations.risk_level: Low (as it is a general statement about the enforcement of rules)
obligations.short_summary: Rules become effective upon publication in Official Gazette.

obligations.obligation:
The change in law events occurred prior to the notification of
these Rules shall be dealt in accordance with the prevalent dispensation /rule
position at the time of occurrence of the event.

The proceedings in the petitions, related to change in law matters, pending
before the Appropriate Commissions, shall be dealt in accordance with
stipulations made in para (ii) above.

For the foregoing reasons, we find the impugned orders of the Central
Commission applying the CIL Rules to matters pending before it for adjudication
under Section 79(1)(f) of Electricity Act on the date of coming into force of said rules
wholly erroneous, improper and bad in law.

The
Central Commission is duty-bound to consider each of them on the merits of the
claims and adjudicate in accordance with law on the dispute(s) in proper exercise of
its jurisdiction under Section 79 of the Electricity Act.

We observe that on 21.02.2022, MoP provided clarification on the CIL Rules, 2021,
stating that changes in law events occurring prior to the notification of the CIL Rules, 2021,
shall be dealt with in accordance with the prevailing dispensation/ rule position at the time of
the event's occurrence.

Also, APTEL vide its judgment dated 05.04.2022 has held that applying
the CIL Rules, 2021 to matters pending for adjudication under Section 79(1)(f) of the
Electricity Act on the date of coming into force of said rules is bad in law.

Per contra, SECI has
submitted that since the notifications dated 30.09.2021 in regard to GST Laws were notified
prior to Change in Law Rules (notified on 22.10.2021), accordingly, these events shall be ‘dealt
in accordance with the prevalent dispensation/rule position at the time of occurrence of t...

However, the Petitioner is required to
establish a one-to-one correlation between the project, the supply of goods, the invoices, and
other relevant documents to prove the payment of the claimed amount due to the change in
law.

We observe that the relevant Articles of the PPA dated 20.08.2021 stipulate as under:
“Law” shall mean in relation to this Agreement all laws in force in India including
Electricity Laws of India and any statute, ordinance, regulation, notification or code,
rule, or any interpretation of any of them...

However, Change in Law shall not include (i) any change in taxes on corporate
income or (ii) any change in any withholding tax on income or dividends
distributed to the shareholders of the SPD.

12.1.3 However, in case of change in rates of safeguard duty, GST and basic customs
duty after 22.06.2020 and resulting in change in Project Cost, then such
change will be treated as ‘Change in Law’ and the quantum of compensation
payment on account of change in rates of such duties and shall be pro...

12.2 Relief for Change in Law
12.2.1 Save and except as provided under Article 12.1.3, the aggrieved Party shall be
required to approach the Hon’ble CERC for seeking approval of Change in
Law.

12.2.2 The decision of the Hon’ble CERC to acknowledge a Change in Law and the
date from which it will become effective, provide relief for the same, shall be
final and governing on SPD and Buying Entity.

12.2.3 In case of Change in Law as approved by the Appropriate Commission pursuant
to Article 12.2.1 or as provided under Article 12.1.3, the SPD/ SECI/ Buying
Entities (as the case may be) shall be entitled for relief as follows:
Every net increase/decrease of Rs. l lakh per MW in the Project Cost ...
